text:
### 1. AMC-Level Information Dataset
AMC Name: SBI Mutual Fund
Established: June 1987
Parent Organization: State Bank of India
Joint Venture Partner: Amundi (France)
Regulator: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
Total Assets Under Management: ₹899084.7 crore
Number of Mutual Fund Schemes: 77
Number of Fund Managers: 36
Chief Executive Officer: Shamsher Singh
Chief Investment Officer: R. Srinivasan
Chief Compliance Officer: Vinaya Datar
Head Office: 9th Floor, Crescenzo, C-38 & 39, G Block, Bandra Kurla Complex, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400051.
Support: 1800 209 3333 | 1800 425 5425 | customer.delight@sbimf.com

### 2. Scheme-Level Factual Details
Scheme: SBI Bluechip Fund
Category: Large Cap Equity Fund
AUM: ₹54821 crore
Expense Ratio: 0.8%
Exit Load: 1% if redeemed within 12 months.
Min SIP: ₹500
Min Lumpsum: ₹5000
Benchmark: Nifty 100
Fund Managers: Sohini Andani, R. Srinivasan
Launch Year: 2006

Scheme: SBI Small Cap Fund
Category: Small Cap Equity Fund
Min SIP: ₹500
Expense Ratio: 0.76%
Exit Load: 1% if redeemed within 1 year.
Fund Manager: R. Srinivasan
Benchmark: Nifty Smallcap 250

### 3. Regulatory Data (SEBI / AMFI Rules)
ELSS Lock-in: 3 years. Deductible up to ₹1.5 lakh under Section 80C.
SEBI Risk Meter: Low, Low to Moderate, Moderate, Moderately High, High, Very High.
Max TER Slab (Equity): 
- Up to ₹500cr: 2.25%
- ₹500-₹750cr: 2.00%
- ₹750-₹2000cr: 1.75%
- ₹2000-₹5000cr: 1.60%
- ₹5000-₹10000cr: 1.50%
- ₹10000-₹50000cr: 1.25%
- Above ₹50000cr: 1.05%

### 4. Operational & Investment Process
Start Investing: 1. Download INDmoney 2. Complete KYC 3. Search SBI Scheme 4. Review Details 5. Choose SIP/Lumpsum 6. Enter Amount 7. Pay via UPI/Netbanking.
SIP Pause: Allowed for up to 3 months.
Liquid Fund Cut-off: 1:30 PM for same-day NAV.
Redemption Timeline: 2-3 working days for equity funds.

### 5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
Q: Can I redeem SBI Mutual Fund units offline?
A: Yes, by visiting the nearest SBI Mutual Fund branch and submitting a redemption request form.
Q: Do I need a demat account?
A: Yes, a demat account is required when investing through platforms like INDmoney.
Q: What documents are required for KYC?
A: Identity proof, address proof, PAN card, and a passport-sized photograph.
